scouting_report: Diminutive Wide Dribbler - Short, light wide players who aggressively carry the ball into the penalty area and attempt many dribbles, but lack physical presence and shot-blocking ability.
Pure Midfield Destroyer - Defensive midfielders who dominate tackling in the middle third and play longer passes, but offer absolutely zero goal or assist threat.
High-Volume Tackler - Midfielders entirely focused on defensive actions, engaging in massive amounts of tackles and challenges, with zero expected attacking output.
Pure Poacher - Highly efficient goalscorers who generate massive xG and non-penalty goals per 90, but have remarkably low overall touches and poor short passing. They exist only to finish.
Old-School Stopper - Traditional penalty-box defenders who excel at clearances and blocking shots, but practically never receive progressive passes or venture into the attacking third.
Possession Metronome - Deep midfielders who dominate the middle third with massive short passing volume and progression to the final third, but pose zero direct scoring threat.
Inefficient Dribbler - Wide players who attempt many take-ons but are frequently tackled and dispossessed. They severely underperform their expected goals and lack total progressive distance.
Diminutive Progressive Passer - Smaller-statured central midfielders who specialize entirely in progressive passing and entering the final third, rather than shooting or physical dominance.
Clinical Set-Piece Threat - Center-backs who take extremely few shots but boast elite conversion rates (Goals/Shot). They rarely miscontrol the ball or touch it in open play in the attacking third.
Elite Creator - Advanced playmakers entirely focused on generating high-quality chances (xA/xAG) for teammates. They are typically shorter and completely avoid defensive penalty area duties.
Press-Resistant Tower - Tall center-backs who boast impeccable medium passing accuracy and high dribble success rates when pressed, rarely miscontrolling the ball.
Pure Goalscorer - Forwards defined entirely by their high volume of actual goals scored. They contribute absolutely nothing to defensive third actions or clearances.
Isolated Shooter - Forwards who take a high volume of shots per 90 but have incredibly low overall touches and pass attempts. When they do dribble, they are frequently tackled.
Two-Way Wide Defender - Traditional fullbacks who excel at swinging crosses into the penalty area and engaging in defensive tackling challenges, but pose zero direct xG threat.
Deep Distributor - Central midfielders characterized by long average passing distances and safe short completions. They have extremely poor shooting efficiency and rarely receive the ball high up.

Build-Up Center Back - Defenders heavily involved in early build-up play, dominating touches in their own third and attempting a high volume of medium-range passes. They almost never receive progressive passes.
Final-Third Wide Creator - Wide players who spend their time exclusively in the attacking zones, producing elite per-90 assists and xAG, while completely avoiding the defensive and middle thirds.
High-xG Target - Forwards who consistently find themselves in positions to generate massive Expected Goals (xG), but are severe liabilities in build-up play with poor passing accuracy.
Traditional Overlapper - Wide defenders who specialize in defensive third tackles and whipping crosses into the box, but are heavily penalized for poor long-passing accuracy and lack of shooting.
Clinical Midfield Finisher - Central midfielders who take very few shots but convert them with ruthless efficiency. They are clean on the ball, boasting high short-pass accuracy.
